skill.name: reference-style-sync
skill.description: One-click synchronization and standardization of reference formats in literature management tools, intelligently fixing metadata errors.
skill.summary: One-click synchronization and standardization of reference formats in literature management tools, intelligently fixing metadata errors.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate academic reference formatting tool. The 87 static findings are predominantly false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' triggers match descriptive text, not actual crypto usage; 'system reconnaissance' triggers match metadata field access in reference parsing; 'path traversal' triggers match security checklist documentation. The remaining medium-severity findings (shell commands, file operations) reflect the skill's intended functionality of running Python scripts to read and write reference files. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or command injection vectors were identified.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 9 locations for weak cryptographic algorithms. All are false positives: the code contains no cryptographic operations. Flags matched on words like 'MD' within class names (MetadataFixer) and descriptive text, not actual crypto usage.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 17 locations for system reconnaissance. All are false positives: flagged lines access metadata fields (id, doi, title, pages, year, authors, journal) from reference documents using standard Python dict .get() calls. This is core reference parsing functionality, not system discovery.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines 294 and 297 in SKILL.md for path traversal. These lines are in a Security Checklist section documenting anti-path-traversal measures. They describe security controls, not vulnerabilities.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md line 7. This is the source attribution link for the skill, displayed in a blockquote. It is not used for any data exfiltration or network calls.

content.user_title: Sync and Standardize Academic Reference Formats
content.value_statement: Academic researchers waste hours manually fixing citation metadata across tools like Zotero and EndNote. This skill automates reference format synchronization to APA, AMA, MLA, Vancouver, and Chicago styles while repairing missing fields like DOIs, author names, and journal abbreviations.
actual_capabilities: Parse and convert references between BibTeX, RIS, CSL JSON, and CSV formats
Normalize author names to standard citation format with proper initials
Standardize journal names using ISO 4 abbreviation rules and a built-in journal mapping library
Repair and normalize DOI, page number, year, and title metadata fields
Detect and remove duplicate references based on DOI or title matching
Generate quality reports identifying missing fields, invalid DOIs, and formatting issues
limitations: Does not connect to live databases or resolve DOIs through external API calls
May not handle non-standard or highly customized citation formats without manual rule additions
Journal name abbreviations follow a built-in rule library and may need updates for niche publications
Metadata repair is based on regex patterns and rules, not database verification

use_cases.title: Format a Zotero Library for Journal Submission
use_cases.description: A researcher exports their Zotero library as BibTeX and needs to format all references to AMA style before submitting to a medical journal.
use_cases.target_user: Medical researcher submitting to AMA-format journals

use_cases.title: Clean Up a Collaborative Reference Database
use_cases.description: A team merges reference files from multiple collaborators into one unified, deduplicated library with consistent formatting.
use_cases.target_user: Research team managing shared citation databases

use_cases.title: Audit Reference Quality Before Paper Submission
use_cases.description: A graduate student runs a quality check on their thesis bibliography to identify missing DOIs, incomplete page numbers, and formatting inconsistencies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Format Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my Zotero library export at [input.bib] to APA style. Save the output as [output.bib].
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple format conversion with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Full Metadata Repair and Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Load [input.bib], repair all metadata including author names, journal names, DOIs, and page numbers. Then convert to AMA format and export to [output.bib].
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete metadata cleanup with style conversion

prompt_templates.title: Deduplicate and Standardize a Merged Library
prompt_templates.prompt: Load [merged_library.json], enable metadata repair and deduplication, convert to Vancouver style, run a quality check, and export both the cleaned library and a quality report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full library cleanup pipeline with duplicate removal

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing with Quality Assurance
prompt_templates.prompt: Process [library.bib] with metadata repair, deduplication, and style conversion to [style]. Then run a quality check and summarize all missing fields, invalid DOIs, and potential duplicate entries found.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pipeline with detailed quality report

output_examples.input: BibTeX file with mixed author name formats and inconsistent journal names
output_examples.output: Uniformly formatted BibTeX file where all author names follow Lastname Initials pattern, journal names are abbreviated per ISO 4, DOIs use consistent doi: prefix, and page numbers use standard hyphen delimiters.

output_examples.input: Zotero CSL JSON export with some incomplete metadata entries
output_examples.output: Quality check report listing 3 entries with missing DOIs, 2 entries with missing page numbers, and 1 duplicate entry. Includes recommendations for manual review of flagged references.

output_examples.input: Multiple RIS files from different collaborators with inconsistent formatting
output_examples.output: Single merged RIS library with duplicates removed, all journal names consistently abbreviated, author names standardized, and page numbers formatted uniformly. All changes are logged per reference.

best_practices: Always back up your original reference library before running any automated repair operations
Review the quality check report after processing to catch edge cases the automated rules may have missed
Specify the target citation style explicitly to ensure accurate field formatting and abbreviation rules
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to verify factual accuracy of citation content such as correct DOIs or page numbers
Do not process sensitive or proprietary reference data in an uncontrolled environment
Do not rely on automated deduplication as the sole method for identifying duplicate references

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports BibTeX (.bib), RIS (.ris), CSL JSON (.json), and CSV (.csv) formats for both input and output.

faq.question: Which citation styles are available?
faq.answer: AMA, APA, MLA, Vancouver, Chicago, and IEEE styles are supported for reference formatting.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to external databases?
faq.answer: No. All metadata repair is performed using built-in rules and regex patterns. It does not resolve DOIs or verify journal names through external API calls.

faq.question: Can I process my entire reference library at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool supports batch processing of 10000 or more entries in a single run.

faq.question: Will the skill modify my original files?
faq.answer: No. The skill reads your input file and writes to a separate output file. Your original files remain unchanged.

faq.question: What metadata fields can be automatically repaired?
faq.answer: Author name format, journal name abbreviation, DOI format normalization, page number delimiters, year format, and title case standardization.
